title: Interactive pocket fold page
subtitle: Adding a folded paper pocket to a scrapbook or journal spread
excerpt: A pocket fold page holds inserts, folded notes, small prints, or cards within the scrapbook itself. The pocket is made from a single strip of paper folded and adhered along three sides, leaving the top open for inserting and removing items. This tutorial covers three pocket formats suited to different positions on the page.
sourceNotes: Original tutorial based on common scrapbooking and interactive journalling techniques.

glossaryTerms.definition: A paper sleeve adhered to a journal or scrapbook page along three sides, leaving one side open to receive inserts. The pocket extends from the page surface and holds flat items without tape or adhesive.

A folded extension of the pocket paper that is glued down to form the adhered base of the pocket. The flap provides a bonding surface that is separate from the pocket face, preventing the adhesive from showing through the front.
A loose item stored within a pocket: a folded note, photograph, printed card, or ticket. An insert should be slightly smaller than the pocket opening to slide in and out without tearing.

content.text: is built from a strip of paper long enough to wrap around the intended
on each side for adhesion. The three formats below work for corner, bottom-edge, and full-page pockets.

items.name: Pocket paper, 120 to 160 gsm
items.notes: Sturdier than the journal page, so the pocket holds its shape. Cut or tear the pocket paper to a size that allows the insert to slide in and out easily.

items.name: Bone folder
items.notes: For scoring and sharpening the flap folds.

items.name: Scissors
items.notes: For trimming the pocket to shape.

items.name: Double-sided tape
items.notes: For adhering the pocket flaps to the page. Double-sided tape gives a cleaner bond than glue stick for paper heavier than 120 gsm.

content.text: Cut a strip of pocket paper the same width as the journal page and approximately 80 mm tall. Score and fold a 15 mm flap along the bottom edge and a 10 mm flap along each side. Apply double-sided tape to the outer face of the bottom flap and both side flaps. Press the pocket onto the page with the open top facing up. Smooth firmly and press under a weight for five minutes.

Cut a square of pocket paper 80 x 80 mm. Fold in half diagonally to make a triangle, with the fold running from the lower left to the upper right corner. Fold a 10 mm flap along the bottom edge and a 10 mm flap along the left edge. Apply double-sided tape to both flaps and press the triangle into the lower left corner of the page. The open corner of the triangle is the pocket entry.

Decorate the pocket before adhering it to the page: it is easier to stamp, draw, or add washi tape to the pocket face while it is flat. Add a label on the outside of the pocket to indicate what the insert holds. The pocket can be made from patterned or decorative paper that contrasts with the journal page background.
